description: Led a distributed engineering organization of 60+ engineers across 10 teams spread across the US, LATAM, and Europe. Coached and developed 10 team leads, including successfully turning around two underperforming leads and establishing clear growth paths.
highlights: Redesigned the engineering interview process to improve candidate evaluation consistency and quality
Partnered with the CISO and led a two-engineer working group to evaluate and implement Snyk, serving as the primary technical driver in strengthening the organization's security posture
Collaborated with a peer Director of Engineering to align on technical vision and overall department direction

description: Founded and led the Growth Engineering team, assembled from existing Canvas engineers to drive revenue-focused initiatives. Team was subsequently tasked with a high-priority security remediation effort, delivering critical fixes across the Canvas codebase.
highlights: Coordinated cross-functionally with service-owning engineering teams and the Customer Success organization to communicate upcoming API changes, ensuring alignment across technical and customer-facing stakeholders

description: Operated as a trusted technical resource within the Canvas organization, sought out for complex cross-team projects, architectural guidance, and high-stakes customer escalations requiring deep product knowledge and creative problem solving.
highlights: Designated technical resource for high-priority customer escalations with the goal of securing new sales or retaining at-risk accounts
Technical Lead and Engineering Representative on a customer load testing initiative, contributing to the successful landing of a new client
Led a cross-product effort to enhance the ease of product demos with pre-populated data, saving the Solutions Engineering team days of manual effort
Optimized expensive database queries identified by DBAs, reducing IOPS costs across multiple projects
Key author of a new company onboarding process for engineers to support a major international hiring surge in Budapest

description: Led five Canvas/edu teams totaling 42 engineers across the United States, Latin America, and Hungary, including a contractor team built from scratch to offload critical services maintenance and free up feature team capacity.
highlights: Led the effort to enhance and refine the bi-annual employee assessment process, resulting in a more precise and consistent experience for Engineering Managers and improved feedback for those being evaluated
Led the engineering component of the annual intern recruitment process, yielding multiple classes of high-performing interns with a 70% hire-to-FTE conversion rate
Guided engineering managers to achieve a quarterly delivery rate of 90% for committed projects
Actively participated in hiring at all levels of the engineering organization across three geographic regions

description: Led four Canvas feature teams totaling 24 engineers, directly managing three team leads and three individual contributors while providing oversight across the full organization.

Led a 9-engineer team across the Chicago office, Salt Lake City office, and remote locations. Operated as a hands-on player/coach, actively contributing to the codebase while representing the company externally at job fairs, meetups, and recruiting events.
highlights: Partnered with the Product Manager to drive open bug count from 50 down to below 5 and maintained that standard going forward

Delivered a major gradebook overhaul ahead of the company's annual conference, shipping key features including a rewritten grade release function, late and missing grade policies, persistent sorting and filtering, and anonymous and moderated grading
Enhanced team productivity by 35% through the introduction of weekly metrics and targeted one-on-one discussions to identify and address impediments

description: Independent contributor on the Gradebook team for the Canvas LMS using Ruby, Rails, JavaScript, React, Redis, and Cassandra. Promoted to Team Lead within two months based on demonstrated leadership skills and a rapid reputation for solving complex, long-standing technical problems.

Led the day-to-day operations and future development of the IT department with a $6M budget and 25 personnel in Chicago and Washington, DC, encompassing servers, databases, telephony, and user endpoint devices.
highlights: Rebuilt the NAR E-Commerce Payment Gateway within six weeks, successfully passing a Level 1 PCI Audit upon completion
Successfully migrated email and calendaring for 300 employees from Lotus Notes/Domino to Microsoft Outlook/Exchange within four weeks
Led the migration of 400+ servers to a virtualized environment, reducing hardware costs, maintenance overhead, and improving redundancy
Led the corporate network redesign to modern firewall design and security standards
Mentored the Managing Director of Application Development, driving the project queue from several months behind schedule to consistent on-time delivery

role: Managing Director, Center for REALTOR® Technology
description: Managed a $2M department of 5 responsible for the advocacy, education, and implementation of technology for the entire NAR membership, while continuing to contribute as a Strategic Architect.
highlights: Brought CRT technology survey development in-house, increasing quality and saving $50,000 annually
Established the "Bloggers' Lounge" at mid-year and annual conferences as a central gathering place and outreach platform for social media savvy members

role: Strategic Architect, Center for REALTOR® Technology
description: Founding member of a specialized department dedicated to the advocacy, education, and implementation of technology for NAR members, contributing as a developer, speaker, and educator across local, state, and national conferences.

Launched and managed NAR's first social media presence, establishing the CRT blog in August 2005

description: Collaborated with early-stage companies to develop their initial internal and external systems.
